assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Case A defines two tasks for stream rhtpa-2.2. Task 1 (upstream backport) has Labels: 'ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123' and Linkage: 'Link to TC-8020 with type "Depend"' (line 68). Task 2 (downstream propagation) has the same three labels (line 75) and Linkage: 'Link to TC-8020 with type "Depend"' (line 118). The cross-stream.md comparison table (line 50) also confirms Case A labels as 'ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-55123' with link type 'Depend'.

assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md (lines 41-45) documents sibling CVE Jira search via JQL returning no results for rhtpa-2.1, concluding 'stream rhtpa-2.1 has no dedicated CVE Jira and requires preemptive remediation (Case B)'. remediation.md (lines 123-125) states 'Stream rhtpa-2.1 is affected (tokio 1.40.0 < 1.42.0) but has no CVE Jira for this CVE. Preemptive remediation tasks are created'. Two preemptive tasks are defined: upstream backport (line 128) and downstream propagation (line 186). cross-stream.md (lines 36-37) confirms 'No sibling CVE Jira exists for this stream, so remediation cannot be tracked through a dedicated Vulnerability issue. Preemptive tasks ensure remediation is not missed.'

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Preemptive Task 1 (line 131): Labels: 'ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive'. Preemptive Task 2 (line 190): Labels: 'ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive'. cross-stream.md table (line 43-44) confirms both preemptive tasks have labels 'ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-55123, security-preemptive'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Preemptive Task 1 (line 182): 'Link to TC-8020 with type "Related" (not "Depend" — different stream)'. Preemptive Task 2 (lines 237-238): 'Link to TC-8020 with type "Related" (not "Depend" — different stream)'. cross-stream.md comparison table (line 51) confirms Case B link type is 'Related' vs Case A 'Depend'. cross-stream.md tasks table (lines 43-44) shows 'Related' link for both preemptive tasks.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Preemptive Task 1 description (lines 135-138): 'Preemptive remediation: This task was created proactively from cross-stream impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream.' Preemptive Task 2 description (lines 193-196): identical prefix referencing TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). cross-stream.md comparison table (line 52) confirms Case B has 'Preemptive remediation notice referencing TC-8020' as description prefix.
